name: security-audit
description: Security audit with vulnerability assessment and remediation

parameters.name: test_command
parameters.description: Command to run tests (defaults to 'pnpm run test')

team.name: auditor
team.role: Security auditor — identifies vulnerabilities, injection vectors, and insecure patterns

team.name: remediator
team.role: Security engineer — applies fixes for identified vulnerabilities without breaking functionality

tasks.title: Audit {{module}} for security vulnerabilities
tasks.description: Perform a security audit of {{module}}. Check for: (1) injection vulnerabilities (SQL, command, path traversal), (2) authentication/authorization flaws, (3) sensitive data exposure, (4) insecure dependencies, (5) input validation gaps, (6) unsafe deserialization. Flag issues at {{severity}} severity or above. Write findings to security-report.md with severity ratings and specific file:line references.

expectations.criteria: The audit is thorough, covers OWASP top 10 categories relevant to the module, and provides specific file:line references for each finding

tasks.title: Remediate security issues in {{module}}
tasks.description: Fix all identified security vulnerabilities in {{module}} at {{severity}} severity or above. For each fix: (1) address the root cause, not just the symptom, (2) add input validation where missing, (3) ensure the fix doesn't break existing functionality. Document each fix in security-fixes.md.

tasks.title: Verify security fixes for {{module}}
tasks.description: Verify all security remediations in {{module}}. For each fix: (1) confirm the vulnerability is resolved, (2) check the fix doesn't introduce new issues, (3) verify no regressions. Write verification results to security-verification.md.

expectations.criteria: All identified vulnerabilities are verified as resolved without introducing new security issues
